days.note: Arrival from Himeji — evening in Hiroshima

activities.name: Check in — Hotel Granvia Hiroshima
activities.notes: Arrive from Himeji. Check in, freshen up.
activities.place: Hotel Granvia Hiroshima South Gate, Hiroshima, Japan

activities.name: Walk to Nagarekawa
activities.notes: 20 min · 1.6 km · Head southwest from the hotel toward the city center

activities.name: 🍴 Dinner — Nagarekawa district
activities.notes: Hiroshima's lively izakaya district — packed lanes of local bars, yakitori, and Japanese pub food. Great first-night spot to get a feel for the city. 10-min walk from hotel.

activities.name: Walk to Shukkeien Garden
activities.notes: 10 min · 0.7 km · Short walk west from the hotel

activities.name: Shukkeien Garden
activities.notes: Beautiful 17th-century Japanese garden, 10-min walk from hotel. Peaceful birthday morning — ponds, stone bridges, traditional tea house. ¥260 at gate.

activities.name: Peace Memorial Park & Museum
activities.notes: A-Bomb Dome, Peace Memorial Museum, Children's Peace Monument. Very moving — allow 2 hours minimum. Book web ticket in advance, queues can exceed 45 min without.
activities.place: Hiroshima Peace Memorial Park, Hiroshima, Japan

activities.name: Hiroshima Orizuru Tower
activities.notes: Open-air observation deck with panoramic views over the city and the Atomic Bomb Dome right below.

activities.name: 🍴 Lunch: Okonomiyaki
activities.notes: Hiroshima-style layered savoury pancakes — completely different from Tokyo-style. Okonomimura is a food building with multiple stalls, pick whichever has the shortest queue.

activities.name: Hiroshima Castle Grounds & Sannomaru
activities.notes: Castle keep closed since March 2026 for structural safety. Walk the moat and grounds (free). Sannomaru area (opened 2025) has archery and samurai tea ceremony experiences.

activities.name: A-Bomb Dome at night
activities.notes: The dome is illuminated at night and looks completely different — stark silhouette against the dark sky, almost total silence. A quiet, moving moment before dinner. Free.

activities.name: 🎂 Birthday Dinner — Wagyu
activities.notes: Pick one of the saved wagyu options above and reserve in advance — mention it's a birthday. Both do table grill with local Hiroshima kuroge wagyu.

activities.name: JR + Ferry to Miyajima
activities.notes: 45 min · JR San'yo Line from Hiroshima Station to Miyajimaguchi (~25 min), then JR Ferry to Miyajima (~10 min) · ¥410 total (JR Pass valid)

activities.name: Itsukushima Shrine
activities.notes: Iconic shrine known for its 'floating' Torii gate during high tide.

activities.name: Omotesando Street & O-Shakushi
activities.notes: Explore street food stalls (grilled oysters are the specialty) and see the World's Largest Rice Scoop (Mega Spoon).

activities.name: Daisho-in Temple
activities.notes: Historic Shingon Buddhist temple complex located at the base of Mount Misen.

activities.name: Henjokutsu Cave
activities.notes: Located on the grounds of Daisho-in. Houses 88 Buddhist statues illuminated by lanterns.

activities.name: Mount Misen Ropeway
activities.notes: ⚡ ADDED: Sacred mountain on Miyajima — ropeway to the top for panoramic views of the Seto Inland Sea. Station is near Daisho-in. ~30 min up + 30 min to explore summit + 30 min down. ¥1,100 one-way / ¥2,000 round trip.

days.note: ⚡ CHANGED: Departure to Onomichi (overnight stop), then Tokyo next day

activities.name: Check out & travel to Onomichi
activities.notes: ⚡ CHANGED: Was direct to Tokyo. Now stopping in Onomichi for an overnight countryside experience. Shinkansen Hiroshima → Mihara (~25 min), then JR to Onomichi (~15 min).
